title: OPEN Stock Drops As Q1 Loss Widens And Revenue Falls
text: OPEN Stock Drops As Q1 Loss Widens And Revenue Falls. Opendoor's stock faced near-term selling pressure following Q1 2026 earnings as investors digested the combination of widening losses and declining revenue. The company reported a GAAP net loss of $173 million, compared to analyst expectations for a narrower loss. Revenue of $720 million, while beating estimates, represented a significant year-over-year decline from the $1.153 billion reported in Q1 2025. The stock's initial post-earnings weakness reflected investor concerns about near-term profitability, despite management's confident messaging about achieving breakeven by year-end 2026. Near-term headwinds include continued operating losses, year-over-year revenue declines, and uncertainty about management's ability to execute on aggressive targets.
